---
title: "Labs using R, <em>UBC Edition</em>"
author:  
output:
  html_document:
    fig_width: 1
---

------------------------------------------------------------------------

**UNDER DEVELOPMENT: Until this statement is removed, consider all pages on this site under development.**

These labs are written for the UBC course BIOL 300: Funamentals of Biostatistics, using resources from *The Analysis of Biological Data* by Whitlock and Schluter. The materials will follow the same order and level as this text, though there may not be one-to-one correspondence between chapters in ABD and tutorials.

These labs have two main functions. First, they are intended to facilitate active learning in some core concepts in introductory statistics. Second, they introduce a key tool in modern statistics, the widely-used, open source statistic program called R.

Before using these labs, download [the zip file containing Data, R scripts, and other resources for these labs](ABDLabs.zip). Remember to start RStudio from the "ABDLabs.Rproj" file in that folder to make these exercises work more seamlessly.

Some of the exercises in these labs (particularly in the first few labs) may require working in a group environment.

<br>

## R tutorials by topic

[0. Loading R and RStudio](00_R_tutorial_Loading_R_and_RStudio.html)

[1. Intro to R](01_R_tutorial_Intro_to_R.html)

[2. Tabular data in R](02_R_tutorial_Tabular_data.html)

[3. Graphics](R_tutorial_Graphics.html)

[4a. Making data files for R](R_tutorial_Making_data_files.html)

[4b. Sampling](R_tutorial_Sampling.html)

[5. Describing data](R_tutorial_Describing_data.html)

[6. Frequency data](R_tutorial_Frequency_data.html)

[7. Contingency analysis](R_tutorial_Contingency_analysis.html)

[8. Normal distribution and sample means](R_tutorial_Normal_and_sample_means.html)

[9. Comparing means of two groups](R_tutorial_Comparing_means_of_2_groups.html)

[10. ANOVA](R_tutorial_ANOVA.html)

[11. Correlation and regression](R_tutorial_Correlation_Regression.html)

[References and data sources for all tutorials](References.html)

<br>

Other resources for Whitlock and Schluter *The Analysis of Biological Data* are [available chapter by chapter here](../index.html).

Comments and suggestions are greatly appreciated. If something is not working or can be improved, please let us know. Please e-mail Vikram Baliga at [vikram.baliga\@ubc.ca](mailto:vikram.baliga@ubc.ca){.email} with any thoughts you have about how these tutorials can be improved.

Instructors, please e-mail <a href="mailto:whitlock@zoology.ubc.ca">Mike Whitlock</a> for a link to some instructor notes on these labs.
